english: heat of the sun, of men, by warm clothing; dros soṅ (dros song) the ground has grown warm, the snow is beginning to melt; drós-na (dros na) when it is getting warm; di gón-na dros lags (di gon na dros lags) if you put that on, you will be warm Mil.; tse γcíg drós-pai gos (tshe gcig dros pa'i gos) warm clothing for one period of existence Mil. — ma-drós-pa (ma dros pa) n. of the Manasarowara or lake of Mapam in Nari. The Hindoos describe it as something like a northern ocean, inhabited by Nagas (v. klu (klu)), and the Tibetans in good faith repeat such fables, at least in their literature, although they know better.
Ld.-Glr. Schl. fol. 17, b., v. lda-mán (lda man).

(Skt. धूति a shaker, agitator?) a word of more recent mystical physiology, 'the middle vein', = dbú-ma (dbu ma) (cf. γtúm-po (gtum po) and tĭg-le (thig le)) Thgr., Mil., Wdn. The Lamas consulted by me asserted, not quite in accordance with books, dhu-ti (dhu ti) to denote a kind of rluṅ (rlung) in the body (which would agree with धू to blow, and with πνεῦμα), a vital power closely connected with the soul, supporting it during lifetime, and leaving it only when separated by death. This would be a new or second signification of dhu-ti (dhu ti), although I cannot vouch for the correctness of the above statement, nor am I able to decide, whether dhu-tí (dhu ti) and à-ba-dhutí (a ba dhu ti) are quite the same. — à-ba-dhuti-pa (a ba dhu ti pa) Tar. 187, 8 is a proper name, Schf.
Hind. धेला, half a paisa, the smallest coin, equal to the tenth part of a penny, W.
1. fut. of _odogs-pa ('dogs pa). — 2. γdags (gdags) the light, day Cs., opp. to sribs (sribs). — 3. in Stg. γdágs-pa (gdags pa) occurs frq. as a translation of प्रज्ञा wisdom.

(ldaṅ? (ldang?)) 1. clothes-stand, rack or rail for hanging up clothes, γdáṅ-la gos _odzár-ba (gdang la gos 'dzar ba), _ogél-ba ('gel ba); γdáṅ-bu (gdang bu) 1. peg or nail, for the same purpose. 2. skás-kyi γdaṅ(-bu) (skas kyi gdang bu) Lex., *s̀ral-dáṅ* (shral dang) W., step of a ladder. — 2. col. for γdeṅ (gdeng).
to open wide, mouth and nostrils, to gape B. and col.; γdáṅ-pai k̄ro-żál (gdang pa'i khro zhal) an angry face with the mouth wide opened Glr.
1. music, harmony, melody, = dbyaṅs (dbyangs), snyags (snyags), also γdaṅs-snyan (gdangs snyan); γdaṅs byéd-pa (gdangs byed pa) to make music C. — 2. resp. for dprál-ba (dpral ba) forehead Cs.

1. v. γdáṅ-ba (gdang ba). 2. resp. one recovering from illness, convalescent, with snyun (snyun), bsnyuṅ-ba (bsnyung ba) Lex.; *ra daṅ* (ra dang) W. he has recovered from his drunken fit, has become sober again.

(Skt. आसन), resp. bżugs-γdan (bzhugs gdan) W., a bolster, or seat composed of several quilts or cushions, put one upon the other (five for common people, nine for people of quality), cf. _obol ('bol); γdan-k̄ri (gdan khri) a throne Glr.; γdan-rábs (gdan rabs) a succession of teachers Tar. 199, 4. The word is much used in polite expressions: γdan _odégs-pa (gdan 'degs pa) to take leave, to withdraw, to depart; γdán-sa (gdan sa) 1. place of residence, bla-mai (bla ma'i) Mil.; dga-ston-gyi (dga' ston gyi) place of a festival Glr. 2. situation, position, rank, ni f., Mil.; γdán-_odren-pa (gdan 'dren pa) to invite, = spyán-_odren-pa (spyan 'dren pa), to appoint, to nominate, dpon-du (dpon du) a chief, a leader Glr.; to go to meet Glr.; *dan-su-c̀e* (dan su ce) W. id.; *dan-kyal-c̀e* (dan kyal ce) W. to accompany, as a mark of attention; dan-p̉eb-pa (dan pheb pa) to arrive Sch.
W., γdam-ṅa (gdam nga) Lex., choice, election, *dám-ka c̀ò-c̀e* (dam ka co ce) W. to choose, to elect; γdám-ṅa byéd-pa (gdam nga byed pa) Lex. id.

fut. of _odóms-pa ('doms pa), γdáms-pa (gdams pa), pf. of _odóms-pa ('doms pa), to advise, rgyál-po-la γdám-pai mdo (rgyal po la gdam pa'i mdo) adviser of kings, a mirror for sovereigns Thgy.; γdáms-pa (gdams pa) sbst. advice, counsel, doctrine, precept, γdáms-ṅág (gdams ngag), W. *γdáms-ka* (gdams ka), γdáms-k̄a* (gdams kha) (cf. k̄á-ta (kha ta), k̄á-lta (kha lta)), resp. żal-γdáms (zhal gdams), bka-γdáms (bka' gdams) id.; γdáms-pa c̀ig żu (gdams pa cig zhu) we ask for some advice Glr.; p̄án-pa γdáms-pa (phan pa gdams pa) a good advice; γdams-ṅág stón-pa (gdams ngag ston pa) Lex., *dám-ka* (dam ka), or k̄á-ta táṅ-c̀e* (kha ta tang ce) W. to give an advice, to advise; γdams-ṅág _odóms-pai tsig (gdams ngag 'doms pa'i tshig) the imperative mood, expressing command or exhortation Gram.
eleg. for _odúg-pa ('dug pa) B. and Khams, 1. to be, to be there, du
